query: What is the current state of the 2026 Maine Senate race between Susan Collins and Democratic candidates? Who is the Democratic nominee or frontrunner, and what do recent head-to-head polls show as of July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research on the 2026 Maine Senate race. Here's the key summary:

**The critical finding:** The race is in an extraordinary state of flux. Susan Collins currently has **no Democratic opponent**. Graham Platner won the June 9 primary but **withdrew July 10, 2026** amid sexual assault allegations. Democrats are picking a replacement via convention (July 25–26, deadline July 27).

**Polling situation (all against the now-defunct Platner):**
- 270toWin average: Platner 48.2% / Collins 47.2%
- Pollsmax weighted average (July 12): Democrat 46.5% / Collins 47.4% (R+0.9)
- Most recent poll (Fox News, 6/30): **Collins 50% / Platner 47%** — showing the race had tightened toward Collins by late June

**New Democratic field:** Troy Jackson (former Senate President, Sanders-backed early frontrunner), Nirav Shah, Shenna Bellows, Jordan Wood, and others. Janet Mills is not re-entering.

**Market/rating signals:** Polymarket's Maine Senate winner market rose to ~63.5% (favoring the Republican/Collins side) after Platner's exit. Cook rates it Toss-up; Inside Elections rates it Tilt Republican.

**Most important caveat for the Aug 31 forecast:** No head-to-head polling exists yet for any new nominee, who won't be chosen until ~July 27 — leaving roughly one month for pollsters to field fresh surveys before the resolution date. A lesser-known replacement may poll differently than Platner did.

sources.snippet: Platner won the Democratic primary over Mills but withdrew following sexual assault accusations; a nominating convention will select a replacement by July 27.

sources.title: 2026 Polls: Maine Senate
sources.snippet: Average of 5 polls: Platner 48.2%, Collins 47.2%. Fox News 6/30: Platner 47/Collins 50; Siena 6/29: 49/47; Wick 6/16: 48/45; Quantus 6/12: 46/45; Tavern 6/8: 51/49.

sources.title: What we know about the process for Maine Democrats to name a new Senate nominee
sources.snippet: Convention July 25-26 in Bangor; declaration deadline July 15; signature deadline July 21; ~600 unpledged delegates; 500 signatures required.

sources.title: Who will replace Graham Platner on the ballot? 7 Democrats to watch
sources.snippet: Troy Jackson (frontrunner, backed by Sanders), Nirav Shah, Shenna Bellows, Jordan Wood, Dan Kleban, Paige Loud, Valli Geiger.

sources.title: Who could replace Graham Platner as Maine's Democratic Senate nominee?
sources.snippet: Multiple Democrats considering the race; Troy Jackson and Nirav Shah named as potential replacements.

sources.title: Maine Poll: Platner Holds Slight Lead over Collins in U.S. Senate Race
sources.snippet: UMass Lowell poll (May 13-26, 2026, 650 LV): Platner 48%, Collins 43%. Gender gap: women Platner 54-35, men Collins 51-42.

sources.title: Susan Collins narrowly edges out Graham Platner in latest Maine Senate race poll
sources.snippet: Fox News poll: Collins 50%, Platner 47% among registered voters.

sources.snippet: Maine Senate winner market ~63.5% on July 13, 2026, up from ~58.5% on July 6.

sources.title: United States Senate election in Maine, 2026
sources.snippet: Race ratings: Cook = Toss-up; Inside Elections = Tilt Republican. General election November 3, 2026.

sources.title: Janet Mills shows no sign of reigniting her Maine Senate bid amid Graham Platner's troubles
sources.snippet: Mills' supporters urged her to reactivate her suspended campaign, but no organized effort emerged.

sources.title: Maine Senate Nomination Process - Maine Democratic Party
sources.snippet: Special Nomination Process with all steps concluding by July 27th at 5 PM.

key_facts: Graham Platner won the June 9, 2026 Democratic primary but withdrew July 10, 2026 amid a sexual assault/rape allegation and other controversies, leaving Democrats with no nominee.
Maine Democrats are choosing a replacement via a nominating convention July 25-26 in Bangor, with a final ballot deadline of July 27, 2026 (5pm); ~600 delegates vote.
As of the forecast horizon, Susan Collins's Democratic opponent is undetermined; all existing head-to-head polls are against the now-defunct Platner.

Troy Jackson (former Maine Senate President, backed by Bernie Sanders/Our Revolution) is the early frontrunner among 7+ candidates including Nirav Shah, Shenna Bellows, Jordan Wood, Dan Kleban, Paige Loud, and Valli Geiger.
Janet Mills, who lost the primary to Platner after suspending in April, has shown no sign of re-entering the race.
270toWin average of 5 June 2026 polls: Platner 48.2% / Collins 47.2% (Platner +1.0).
Pollsmax weighted polling average (updated July 12, 2026, 26 polls): Democrat 46.5% / Republican 47.4% (R +0.9).
Fox News poll (6/30/2026) showed Collins 50% - Platner 47%, the most recent major poll, indicating the race tightened toward Collins by late June.
Race ratings: Cook Political Report = Toss-up; Inside Elections = Tilt Republican.
Polymarket 'Maine Senate Election Winner' market ~63.5% on July 13, 2026 (favoring the Republican/Collins side), up from ~58.5% on July 6 before Platner's withdrawal.
Democrats have not won a Maine U.S. Senate seat since 1988; Collins is the only GOP senator representing a state Trump never carried.
No head-to-head polling yet exists for any potential new Democratic nominee against Collins; the nominee won't be set until ~July 27, 2026.
